content: For me, it was the first time since early first grade that I had been in public school, full stop. The change was quite drastic for me, having been in a small private school, which included K-12, and at the most, a couple or 300 students, full stop. In my new school, my ninth grade class itself had over 400 students, so I felt a little bit lost as far as not knowing any of them and being a newcomer, full stop. During my high school years, I started acting up, so to speak, to the chagrin of my parents, full stop.I did my first drinking during these years, despite maintaining above-average curricular grades and participating in marching band during my first two years of high school and track and field and cross-country as a sports participant during my 11th and 12th grade years.
